svg.title: JavaScript data types overview
svg.desc: JavaScript has two main categories: Primitive types (String, Number, Boolean, Undefined, Null, BigInt, Symbol) and Non-Primitive types (Object, Array, Function)

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What is a data type in JavaScript?')
sendPrompt('What is a primitive data type in JavaScript?')
sendPrompt('What is a non-primitive data type in JavaScript?')
sendPrompt('Explain String data type with examples')
sendPrompt('Explain Number data type with examples')
sendPrompt('Explain Boolean data type with examples')
sendPrompt('Explain Undefined and Null in JavaScript')
sendPrompt('Explain Object data type in JavaScript with examples')
sendPrompt('Explain Array data type in JavaScript with examples')
sendPrompt('Explain Function data type in JavaScript with examples')

svg.text: Click any box to learn more
